children.label: A
children.text: to export, attempt to export, or otherwise transport from the United States any Item Prohibited from Exportation;

children.label: B
children.text: to conspire with any person to engage in an activity described in subparagraph (A); or

children.label: C
children.text: to conceal an activity described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 2 Penalties
children.text: Any person who violates paragraph (1) and knows, or in the exercise of due care should have known, that the Item Prohibited from Exportation was taken, possessed, transported, or sold in violation of, or in a manner unlawful under, any Federal law or treaty, shall be fined in accordance with , imprisoned for not more than 1 year and 1 day for a first violation, and not more than 10 years for a second or subsequent violation, or both.section 3571 of title 18

children.label: i
children.text: detain any Item Prohibited from Exportation that is exported, attempted to be exported, or otherwise transported from the United States in violation of paragraph (1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: deliver the Item Prohibited from Exportation to the Secretary.

children.heading: Forfeiture
children.label: B Forfeiture
children.text: Any Item Prohibited from Exportation that is exported, attempted to be exported, or otherwise transported from the United States in violation of paragraph (1) shall be subject to forfeiture to the United States in accordance with chapter 46 of title 18 (including section 983(c) of that chapter).

children.label: i
children.text: the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act ( et seq.) (including , as added by that Act); or25 U.S.C. 3001section 1170 of title 18

children.label: ii
children.text: the Archaeological Resources Protection Act of 1979 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 470aa

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No Item Requiring Export Certification may be exported from the United States without first having obtained an export certification in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: aa
children.text: “cultural items” in section 2 of the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act (); and25 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: bb
children.text: “archaeological resource” in section 3 of the Archaeological Resources Protection Act of 1979 ();16 U.S.C. 470bb

children.label: aa
children.text: cultural items under ; andsection 1170(b) of title 18

children.label: bb
children.text: archaeological resources under subsections (b) and (c) of section 6 of Archaeological Resources Protection Act of 1979 ();16 U.S.C. 470ee

children.label: aa
children.text: include the definitions of the terms “Native American” and “Native Hawaiian” in section 2 of the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act (); and25 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: bb
children.text: describe how those terms apply to archaeological resources under this chapter; and

children.label: aa
children.text: an export certification is required only for Items Requiring Export Certification; and

children.label: bb
children.text: fair notice is given to exporters and other persons regarding which items require an export certification under this subsection; and

children.label: I
children.text: an item made solely for commercial purposes is presumed to not qualify as an Item Requiring Export Certification, unless an Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization challenges that presumption; and

children.label: II
children.text: in some circumstances, receipts or certifications issued by Indian Tribes or Native Hawaiian organizations with a cultural affiliation with an item may be used as evidence to demonstrate a particular item does not qualify as an Item Requiring Export Certification.

children.label: A
children.text: the Item Requiring Export Certification is not under ongoing Federal investigation;

children.label: B
children.text: the export of the Item Requiring Export Certification would not otherwise violate any other provision of law; and

children.label: i
children.text: is not an Item Prohibited from Exportation;

children.label: ii
children.text: was excavated or removed pursuant to a permit issued under section 4 of the Archaeological Resources Protection Act of 1979 () and in compliance with section 3(c) of the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act (), if the permit for excavation or removal authorizes export; or16 U.S.C. 470cc25 U.S.C. 3002(c)

children.label: I
children.text: the exporter has a right of possession (as defined in section 2 of the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act ()) of the Item Requiring Export Certification; or25 U.S.C. 3001

children.label: II
children.text: the Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization has relinquished title or control of the Item Requiring Export Certification in accordance with section 3 of the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act ().25 U.S.C. 3002

children.heading: Export certification application and issuance procedures
children.label: 3 Export certification application and issuance procedures

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An exporter seeking to export an Item Requiring Export Certification from the United States shall submit to the Secretary an export certification application in accordance with clause (iii).

children.label: I
children.text: subject the exporter to criminal penalties pursuant to ; andsection 1001 of title 18

children.label: II
children.text: prohibit the exporter from receiving an export certification for any Item Requiring Export Certification in the future unless the exporter submits additional evidence in accordance with subparagraph (B)(iii)(I).

children.heading: Form of export certification application
children.label: iii Form of export certification application
I
children.text: describe, and provide pictures of, each Item Requiring Export Certification that the applicant seeks to export;

children.label: II
children.text: include all available information regarding the provenance of each such Item Requiring Export Certification; and

children.label: III
children.text: include the attestation described in subparagraph (B)(i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In completing an export certification application with respect to an Item Requiring Export Certification that the exporter seeks to export, the exporter shall attest that, to the best of the knowledge and belief of the exporter, the exporter is not attempting to export an Item Prohibited from Exportation.

children.heading: Sufficiency of attestation
children.label: ii Sufficiency of attestation
children.text: An attestation under clause (i) shall be considered to be sufficient evidence to support the application of the exporter under subparagraph (A)(iii)(III), on the condition that the exporter is not required to provide additional evidence under clause (iii)(I).

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The Secretary shall give notice to an exporter that submits an export certification application under subparagraph (A)(i) that the exporter is required to submit additional evidence in accordance with subclause (III) if the Secretary has determined under subparagraph (A)(ii) that the exporter made a willful or knowing false statement on the application or any past export certification application.

children.label: aa
children.text: delayed pursuant to the examination by the Secretary of the eligibility of the Item Requiring Export Certification for an export certification; or

children.label: bb
children.text: denied by the Secretary because the Secretary determined that the Item Requiring Export Certification is not eligible for an export certification under this subsection.

children.heading: Additional evidence
children.label: III Additional evidence
children.text: On receipt of notice under subclause (I), an exporter shall, or on receipt of a notice under subclause (II), an exporter may, provide the Secretary with such additional evidence as the Secretary may require to establish that the Item Requiring Export Certification is eligible for an export certification under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and maintain a secure central Federal database information system (referred to in this subparagraph as the “database”) for the purpose of making export certification applications available to Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian organizations.

children.heading: Collaboration required
children.label: ii Collaboration required
children.text: The Secretary shall collaborate with Indian Tribes, Native Hawaiian organizations, and the interagency working group convened under in the design and implementation of the database.section 3075(a) of this title

children.heading: Availability
children.label: iii Availability
children.text: Immediately on receipt of an export certification application, the Secretary shall make the export certification application available on the database.

children.heading: Deletion from database
children.label: iv Deletion from database
children.text: On request by an Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization, the Secretary shall delete an export certification application from the database.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: v Technical assistance
children.text: If an Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization lacks sufficient resources to access the database or respond to agency communications in a timely manner, the Secretary, in consultation with Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian organizations, shall provide technical assistance to facilitate that access or response, as applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: On receipt of an export certification application for an Item Requiring Export Certification that meets the requirements of subparagraphs (A) and (B), if the Secretary, in consultation with Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian organizations with a cultural affiliation with the Item Requiring Export Certification, determines that the Item Requiring Export Certification is eligible for an export certification under paragraph (2), the Secretary may issue an export certification for the Item Requiring Export Certification.

children.label: I
children.text: the Secretary shall have 1 business day to notify the relevant Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian Organizations of an application for export of an Item Requiring Export Certification;

children.label: II
children.text: Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian organizations shall have 9 business days to review the export certification application;

children.label: III
children.text: if an Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization notifies the Secretary that the Item Requiring Export Certification may not be eligible for an export certification under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall have 7 business days to review the application;

children.label: IV
children.text: if no Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization so notifies the Secretary, the Secretary shall have 1 business day to review the application;

children.label: V
children.text: with notice to the exporter, the Secretary may extend the review of an application for up to 30 business days if credible evidence is provided that the Item Requiring Export Certification may not be eligible for an export certification under paragraph (2); and

children.label: VI
children.text: the Secretary shall make a determination to approve or deny the export certification application within the time allotted.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If credible evidence is provided that indicates that an item that received an export certification under subparagraph (D) is not eligible for an export certification under paragraph (2), the Secretary may immediately revoke the export certification.

children.heading: Determination
children.label: ii Determination
children.text: In determining whether a revocation is warranted under clause (i), the Secretary shall consult with Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian organizations with a cultural affiliation with the affected Item Requiring Export Certification.

children.label: i
children.text: detain any Item Requiring Export Certification that an exporter attempts to export or otherwise transport without an export certification; and

children.label: ii
children.text: deliver the Item Requiring Export Certification to the Secretary, for seizure by the Secretary.

children.heading: Forfeiture
children.label: B Forfeiture
children.text: Any Item Requiring Export Certification that is detained under subparagraph (A)(i) shall be subject to forfeiture to the United States in accordance with chapter 46 of title 18 (including section 983(c) of that chapter).

children.heading: Repatriation or return to exporter
children.label: C Repatriation or return to exporter

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of delivery to the Secretary of an Item Requiring Export Certification under subparagraph (A)(ii), the Secretary shall determine whether the Item Requiring Export Certification is an Item Prohibited from Exportation.

children.label: I
children.text: the Native American Graves Protection and Repatriation Act ( et seq.) (including , as added by that Act); or25 U.S.C. 3001section 1170 of title 18

children.label: II
children.text: the Archaeological Resources Protection Act of 1979 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 470aa

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If the Secretary determines that credible evidence does not establish that the Item Requiring Export Certification is an Item Prohibited from Exportation, or if the Secretary does not complete the determination by the deadline described in clause (i), the Secretary shall return the Item Requiring Export Certification to the exporter.

children.heading: Effect
children.label: II Effect
children.text: The return of an Item Requiring Export Certification to an exporter under subclause (I) shall not mean that the Item Requiring Export Certification is eligible for an export certification under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any person to export, attempt to export, or otherwise transport from the United States any Item Requiring Export Certification without first obtaining an export certification.

children.label: I
children.text: assessed a civil penalty in accordance with such regulations as the Secretary promulgates pursuant to ; andsection 3078 of this title

children.label: II
children.text: subject to any other applicable penalties under this chapter.

children.heading: Items prohibited from exportation
children.label: B Items prohibited from exportation
children.text: Whoever exports an Item Prohibited from Exportation without first securing an export certification shall be liable for a civil money penalty, the amount of which shall equal the total cost of storing and repatriating the Item Prohibited from Exportation.

children.label: i
children.text: to process export certification applications under this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to store and repatriate the Item Prohibited from Exportation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any person who attempts to export or otherwise transport from the United States an Item Requiring Export Certification without first obtaining an export certification, but voluntarily returns the Item Requiring Export Certification, or directs the Item Requiring Export Certification to be returned, to the appropriate Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization in accordance with prior to the commencement of an active Federal investigation shall not be prosecuted for a violation of subparagraph (A) with respect to the Item Requiring Export Certification.section 3074 of this title

children.heading: Actions not commencing a Federal investigation
children.label: ii Actions not commencing a Federal investigation
I
children.text: The submission by the exporter of an export certification application for the Item Requiring Export Certification under paragraph (3)(A)(i).

children.label: II
children.text: The detention of the Item Requiring Export Certification by the Secretary of Homeland Security, acting through the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection, under paragraph (4)(A)(i).

children.label: III
children.text: The delivery to the Secretary of the Item Requiring Export Certification by the Secretary of Homeland Security, acting through the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection, under paragraph (4)(A)(ii).

children.label: IV
children.text: The seizure by the Secretary of the Item Requiring Export Certification under paragraph (4)(A)(ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may assess reasonable fees to process export certification applications under this subsection, subject to subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Availability of amounts collected
children.label: B Availability of amounts collected
children.text: Fees authorized under subparagraph (A) shall be collected and available only to the extent and in the amounts provided in advance in appropriations Acts.

children.heading: Administrative appeal
children.label: 7 Administrative appeal
children.text: If the Secretary denies an export certification or an Item Requiring Export Certification is detained under this subsection, the exporter, on request, shall be given a hearing on the record in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Secretary promulgates pursuant to .section 3078 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary, the Secretary of State, the Attorney General, and the heads of all other relevant Federal agencies shall require all appropriate personnel to participate in training regarding applicable laws and consultations to facilitate positive government-to-government interactions with Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian Organizations.

children.heading: U.S. Customs and Border Protection training
children.label: B U.S. Customs and Border Protection training
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security, acting through the Commissioner of U.S. Customs and Border Protection, shall require all appropriate personnel of U.S. Customs and Border Protection to participate in training provided by the Secretary of the Interior or an Indian Tribe or Native Hawaiian organization to assist the personnel in identifying, handling, and documenting in a culturally sensitive manner Items Requiring Export Certification for purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: C Consultation
children.text: In developing or modifying and delivering trainings under subparagraphs (A) and (B), the applicable heads of Federal agencies shall consult with Indian Tribes and Native Hawaiian organizations.

children.heading: Agreements to request return from foreign countries
children.label: c Agreements to request return from foreign countries

children.text: to discourage commerce in, and collection of, Items Prohibited from Exportation;
to encourage the voluntary return of tangible cultural heritage; and
to expand the market for the products of Indian art and craftsmanship in accordance with .section 305a of this title
